what_it_does: Documents the conventions, vocabulary-mirroring contract, and rules for the db/ persistence layer in the Counterpart project.
observations: db/ is described as the durable persistence layer, with root CLAUDE.md conventions applying.
schema.sql is the single Postgres 15+ schema definition and requires pgvector (384-dim local embeddings) and pgcrypto (UUIDs).

The database is framed as a durable shadow of front-end registries, with a table mapping DB objects to MS_STATUS, MS_KINDS, MS_RESOLVE, MS_TOKENS/MS_DECISIONS, MS_MEMORY, MS_STREAMS, and MS_RESOLVE.ORDER.

Rules require one vocabulary across DB enums/seeds and front-end registries, universal type (kind) to instance (artifact) resolution, local-only embeddings, auditable learning with origin/weight on every memory row, and seed.sql generated from the registries.
Memory recall uses cosine distance (<=>) multiplied by weight through recall_memory().
standouts: The DB is explicitly not a separate model but a mirror of front-end registries.
schema.sql is the ONE definition of the database.
Embeddings are local-only, fixed at 384 dimensions, with cosine recall.
Seed data must be generated from registries rather than hand-maintained.
conventions: Single source of truth and no duplication.
Vocabulary lockstep between DB and front-end registries.
concerns: seed.sql is mentioned as not yet created, creating a pending dependency on a generation pipeline.
Embedding dimension 384 is hardcoded in both schema and registry, requiring coordinated changes.
Vocabulary drift between DB enums/seeds and front-end registries could silently break resolution and rendering.
notes: This file defines the db/ layer as a registry-mirrored, auditable persistence substrate with local embeddings and strict vocabulary parity.
questions: What is the exact mechanism or script that will generate seed.sql from the registries?
How is vocabulary drift between DB and front-end registries detected or enforced?
What does loud failure look like for the persistence layer in practice?
Are there migration or versioning rules for schema.sql beyond being the single source?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it is a convention and vocabulary guide for the database layer, exactly as the AGENTS — db/ heading states.
intention_signals: It tries to prevent divergence between the database schema and front-end registries by making the DB a faithful, auditable shadow of front-end vocabularies, with local embeddings and generated seeds.
novelty: It treats the database as a mirror of front-end registries rather than an independent model, and mandates local-only 384-dim embeddings with generated seed data.
contribution: It establishes the db/ layer's role, vocabulary-lockstep rules, local-embedding policy, and auditable-memory policy, which a fused system would preserve as the persistence-layer contract.
summary_for_embedding: AGENTS.md for db/ documents the Counterpart database layer as a Postgres 15+ schema that mirrors front-end registries, uses local 384-dim pgvector embeddings, and enforces single-source vocabulary parity and auditable provenance.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to keep the database schema, seed data, and front-end registries in lockstep while mandating local embeddings and auditable learning.
